//: Step-A typecheck pilot. tsc --noEmit ONLY — NEVER emits JS; the browser keeps loading the raw .js. Type-checking is OPT-IN per file via a `// @ts-check` comment (checkJs:false), so today only api.js + admin-access-codes.js are checked. Two-way door: delete this file and the `// @ts-check` comments become inert. JSDoc comments only in .js — never inline TS syntax (would break the browser + the vm-sandbox tests).
//strict: strict:false is DELIBERATE for the pilot. strict:true on a real loose controller = ~170 errors/module (73 strictNullChecks DOM-null + 29 noImplicitAny params) that DROWN the field-drift signal and make per-module typing expensive. strict:false still delivers the pilot's value — property-existence checks (TS2339) catch a BE field rename at the call site (drift-catch) — at near-zero per-module cost. Revisit strict:true as a later, separately-scoped step.
